reasoning: Short-term momentum turned negative sharply on Feb 11-13, as Brent dropped from ~$71.50 to ~$69.90 in response to a heavily bearish EIA forecast projecting an average $58/bbl in 2026 due to oversupply. The market exhibits backwardation (futures trading notably lower than spot), strongly indicating an expected downward glide path for spot prices. This yields a primary downward drift in the short term, pulling the probability of a price increase below 50%. For the 1-week horizon (Feb 22), the recent bearish momentum and backwardation strongly hold, giving ~0.42. For the 1-month horizon (Mar 17), random walk dynamics and geopolitical tail risks increasingly dilute the directional signal, so we follow the structural instruction to regress long-horizon forecasts toward maximum uncertainty (0.5), yielding ~0.47.
